questions.q: Of which kingdom was Dasharatha the king?
questions.explanation: Dasharatha ruled the kingdom of Kosala, whose capital was Ayodhya.

questions.q: What was the capital city of Dasharatha's kingdom?
questions.explanation: Ayodhya was the capital of Kosala, ruled by Dasharatha.

questions.q: Which of his sons was Dasharatha's favorite and chosen heir?
questions.explanation: Rama, the eldest son, was Dasharatha's beloved son and chosen to be crowned heir.

questions.q: How many sons did Dasharatha have in total?
questions.explanation: Dasharatha had four sons: Rama, Bharata, Lakshmana, and Shatrughna.

questions.q: Which queen demanded that Rama be exiled?
questions.explanation: Queen Kaikeyi demanded Rama's exile using the two boons Dasharatha had promised her.

questions.q: For how many years was Rama to be exiled according to Kaikeyi's demand?
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi demanded that Rama be exiled to the forest for fourteen years.

questions.q: Who did Kaikeyi want to be crowned king in Rama's place?
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi used her boons to have her own son Bharata crowned king instead of Rama.

questions.q: What happened to Dasharatha soon after Rama left for exile?
options: He renounced the throne and became a hermit
He fell into a long sleep
questions.explanation: Overcome with grief at his beloved son's exile, Dasharatha died shortly afterward.

questions.q: Which divine ritual did Dasharatha perform to obtain sons?
questions.explanation: Dasharatha performed the Putrakameshti sacrifice (alongside the Ashvamedha) to be blessed with sons.

questions.q: Dasharatha is described in the card as the father of which hero?
questions.explanation: Dasharatha was the father of Rama, the central hero of the Ramayana.

questions.q: How many queens (chief wives) did Dasharatha have?
questions.explanation: Dasharatha had three chief queens: Kaushalya, Kaikeyi, and Sumitra.

questions.q: Which queen was the mother of Lakshmana and Shatrughna?
questions.explanation: Sumitra was the mother of the twins Lakshmana and Shatrughna.

questions.q: On what occasion had Kaikeyi earlier earned the two boons from Dasharatha?
options: When she nursed him back to health after illness
When she saved him on a battlefield against the asuras
When she won a chariot race for him
When she defeated a rival queen in debate
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi had saved Dasharatha's life during a battle aiding the gods against the asuras, for which he granted her two boons.

questions.q: Which sage's curse foretold that Dasharatha would die grieving for his son?
questions.explanation: The blind parents of Shravana Kumara, whom Dasharatha killed by accident, cursed him to die in grief over a son.

questions.q: How did Dasharatha accidentally kill the youth Shravana Kumara?
options: With a sword in a duel
By mistaking him for an animal while hunting by sound
By trampling him with his chariot
questions.explanation: Hunting by sound at night, Dasharatha shot an arrow toward the noise of Shravana filling water, mistaking him for an animal.

questions.q: Who was the chief preceptor and royal priest of Dasharatha's court?
questions.explanation: Sage Vasishtha was the family priest (rajaguru) and chief preceptor of Dasharatha's royal house.

questions.q: Which sage requested Dasharatha to send Rama to protect a sacrifice from demons?
questions.explanation: Sage Vishwamitra came to Ayodhya and asked Dasharatha to send Rama to guard his sacrifice from rakshasas.

questions.q: What was Dasharatha's reaction when Vishwamitra first asked for Rama?
options: He was reluctant and pained to part with his young son
He refused outright and was cursed
questions.explanation: Dasharatha was deeply reluctant to send the young Rama, but Vasishtha persuaded him to honor the sage's request.

questions.q: To which dynasty (royal line) did Dasharatha belong?
questions.explanation: Dasharatha was a king of the Solar dynasty, the Ikshvaku line of Suryavamsha.

questions.q: Whom did Dasharatha send to bring Bharata home after the king's death drew near?
options: No one; messengers were sent only after his death
questions.explanation: Bharata was away at his maternal grandfather's kingdom, and messengers were dispatched to fetch him only after Dasharatha had died.

questions.q: What was the name of Dasharatha's trusted charioteer and minister?
questions.explanation: Sumantra was Dasharatha's devoted charioteer and counselor who drove Rama toward the forest.

questions.q: Where did Kaikeyi confront Dasharatha to demand her boons?
options: In the anger-chamber (kopa-bhavana) where she lay on the floor
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi withdrew to the kopa-bhavana, the chamber of wrath, where she lay on the bare floor and made her demands.

questions.q: What is the most commonly cited meaning of the name 'Dasharatha'?
options: One of ten chariots / whose chariot moves in ten directions
questions.explanation: 'Dasharatha' is traditionally interpreted as 'ten chariots' or one whose chariot can move in all ten directions, reflecting his martial skill.

questions.q: Whose handmaid incited Kaikeyi to turn against Rama?
questions.explanation: The hunchbacked maidservant Manthara poisoned Kaikeyi's mind, urging her to demand Rama's exile and Bharata's coronation.

questions.q: Who was the father of Kaushalya, Dasharatha's eldest queen, according to tradition?
questions.explanation: Kaushalya is traditionally said to be a princess of Kashi (often linked with the Kosala royal house by marriage).

questions.q: From which kingdom did Kaikeyi come, making her father the king of that land?
questions.explanation: Kaikeyi was the princess of Kekaya, daughter of King Ashvapati of the Kekaya kingdom.

questions.q: Which adopted daughter of Dasharatha was married to the sage Rishyasringa?
questions.explanation: Shanta, Dasharatha's daughter (given in adoption to King Romapada), was married to the sage Rishyasringa who later performed Dasharatha's sacrifice.

questions.q: What divine substance, emerging from the Putrakameshti sacrifice, did Dasharatha distribute among his queens?
options: A golden vessel of divine payasa (sweet rice pudding)
questions.explanation: A divine being rose from the sacrificial fire bearing a vessel of payasa, which Dasharatha divided among his queens, leading to the birth of his sons.

questions.q: When dying, for whose return did Dasharatha cry out in his final moments?
questions.explanation: In his last moments Dasharatha lamented and called out for Rama, dying of grief over his exiled son.

questions.q: What happened to Dasharatha's body while Bharata was away after the king died?
options: It was preserved in a vat of oil until Bharata returned
It was buried by the river
It was carried to the forest to find Rama
questions.explanation: Because Bharata was absent, Dasharatha's body was preserved in a trough of oil (taila) so the last rites could await the heir's return.
